free_text: Cross sections and recoil properties in the
The ZGS at Argonne National Laboratory.
The target stacks consisted of 20 um depleted uranium
foil surrounded by three layers of 20 um high-purity
(99.999%) aluminium. The innermost foils served as
recoil catchers, the middle foils as beam monitors,
and the outer ones as guard foils. In the case of the
300-GeV bombardments, the target stacks were sealed
in evacuated plastic bags in order to prevent the air
layer between target and catcher foils.
A calibrated 70 cm3 Ge(LI) detector or thin
The listed values have been corrected for secondary
effects on both the uranium and monitor reactions.
The actual uncertainties in the cross sections
include in addition to the listed standard deviations
the errors in branching ratios, detector efficiencies,
and cross sections of the monitor reactions.
The experimental standard deviations are based
on the indicated number of replicate determination.
Fluorescence yield and k-capture branch. An incorrect
abundance value was given by K.Beg and Porile (see
REL-REF) for this nuclide. However, the correct value
was used in the computation of the cross section.
